capital._redirect_note: Owner preference: while speculative is in LEARNING mode (no live spec buy this month), DON'T leave the $50 idle — recommend deploying it into Core or Growth this month (agent picks the better spot, leaning Core/VOO for safety unless there's a strong Growth conviction). Still TEACH one speculative setup to study. Money working > an idle opportunities fund.
capital._note: Owner starts at $500/mo (first buy by end of June 2026) and will scale toward $1,000/mo, building slowly. Each run, read monthly_investment_usd_current AND the actual portfolio size, then split by the 70/20/10 allocation (so $500 -> 350/100/50; $1000 -> 700/200/100) and ADAPT emphasis as the portfolio grows. $200/mo income stays a Year-2/3 target once the speculative bucket is real money — NOT Year-1. Until the opportunities fund reaches $500, speculative ideas are LEARNING/paper-trade only.

deployment.core_mix_note: Default Core DCA split (config-driven). Mostly VOO (US S&P 500) + a small VXUS (international) + SCHD (dividend) tilt — justified in the monthly brief so it isn't 'why only VOO'. Owner may set pure VOO ({"VOO":1.0}). Core is AUTO-DCA: buy the fixed monthly Core amount across this mix every month regardless of price; lean MORE on red days.

dry_powder._note: Growth + speculative money is HELD AS CASH (dry powder), tracked in the dry_powder Postgres table per month (columns month, growth_available, spec_available, rolled_months). Deploy ONLY when a candidate clears the Rigorous-Mode gate (Medium+ conviction, risk gate passed) AND price is inside its entry zone — NOT just because a new month started. If no qualifying setup appears, growth cash waits up to rollover_months (2); after that, suggest DCA'ing it into Core so money isn't idle forever.

entry_zones._note: Every buy idea carries a buy ZONE (entry_zone_low/high), a VALID-UNTIL (default 5 trading days, or a stated condition like 'until it closes above $215'), and an INVALIDATION/stop (= the Bear-Case invalidation level). Late-look-friendly: the 10:30/13:30 intraday checks re-evaluate open zones against the live price and tell the owner if he's still in range. All three are persisted on the suggestions row (entry_zone_low, entry_zone_high, valid_until, invalidation_level).

schedule._cadence_note: Owner chose a DAILY weekday brief (07:30 ET) for the learning habit. Actionable money moves stay MONTHLY (full block on the first brief of the calendar month; a 1-line reminder otherwise). Free data APIs are fine — Alpha Vantage's 25/day budget resets each day. Cost: daily uses ~5x the Claude (Pro) usage of weekly; no separate API bill. v2 adds intraday + post-market runs (see cadence block).

runs.note: Full brief; monthly-plan on the 1st weekday of the month, else daily-status. Runs the full Rigorous-Mode pipeline (scan + watchlist + debates + scoring).
Scoped to open entry-zones + current holdings ONLY. Message only if a buy zone triggered or a holding hit invalidation. Silent otherwise.
Post-market analysis: record how each watched/held name behaved -> stock_observations + daily_snapshots; append a lessons.md regime line. Quiet unless something needs the owner.

cadence._note: Owner-chosen weekday cadence (Central time; 06:30 CT = 07:30 ET). Intraday (10:30/13:30) and post-market (15:10) runs are TOKEN-LEAN: they read only the relevant slice (open zones + holdings), so total cadence is ~2.5-3x one full run, not 4x. Start on Pro, measure ~2 weeks, then tune dials (drop to morning + 1 intraday + EOD) or upgrade to Max only if tight.

email.note: optional fallback; needs Gmail sign-in, may not work in scheduled cloud runs

data._note: DATA SWAP (2026-06-17 Rigorous Mode): yfinance is now PRIMARY (quotes, full price history, fundamentals, predefined screeners — no hard daily cap). Technical indicators (RSI-14, MACD 12/26/9, SMA/EMA 50 & 200) are COMPUTED LOCALLY in-session from yfinance price history, removing the old Alpha Vantage indicator dependency. Finnhub is SECONDARY (stock/metric fundamentals, company news+sentiment, earnings dates, insider Form 4; 60/min free). Alpha Vantage is an OPTIONAL last-resort BACKUP only (its 25/day cap was the binding limit, so it is demoted). Works in a cloud schedule (no local server).

depth._note: FULL multi-round debate runs on money-moves (the month's growth pick + 2-3 runner-ups), any buy/trim/sell, any watchlist promote/retire, and EVERY name the owner holds (portfolio.json). COMPACT one-round structured pass (1 bull / 1 bear / 1 risk flag, reusing data already pulled, no extra API calls) runs on every other watchlist + scan-shortlist name. Nothing is skipped; depth concentrates where real money is at stake.

confidence_gate._note: A BUY is suggested only at Medium-or-higher conviction AFTER the debate. Low conviction is demoted to 'watch', never suggested as a buy.

rigor._note: RIGOROUS MODE (spec 2026-06-17): formalizes the TradingAgents-style deliberation already used loosely. Each analyzed name gets a structured, logged Bull-vs-Bear + risk-veto pass; the risk gate can override the debate (veto/downgrade). Full-depth names additionally run three reel-sourced checklists (Deep Dive / Peer relative-valuation / Bear Case). Suggestion-only and brief format are UNCHANGED — all of this runs behind the scenes and is enriched into data/suggestions-log.jsonl.

market_scan.method: broad free screeners narrow the whole market to a shortlist, then deep-analyze only the shortlist + watchlist (respects API rate limits). NEVER iterate the full ticker list.

universe_filters.note: Quality/liquidity floor — removes penny stocks & illiquid junk before scoring. Eliminates most noise and keeps the scan cheap. (~$2B = mid-cap and up.)

radar.note: DYNAMIC watchlist (owner opted into autonomous management). The agent self-curates data/radar.json from scanner/news finds, then AUTO-PROMOTES persistently-strong names into watchlist.json and RETIRES stale/broken ones it previously added — reporting every change in the brief (transparent + reversible). It NEVER removes a name the owner holds or manually added (only names IT added), respects watchlist_max_per_bucket, and the owner can veto/revert anytime.

learning._note: The agent keeps a running 'learning memory': distilled lessons + trend observations (e.g. 'my biotech calls underperform', 'growth picks do better bought on red days', 'market regime shifted from X to Y'). It READS this back every run and APPLIES it, and UPDATES it with new lessons + a compare of the latest trend vs the prior trend. v2: structured grades live in the suggestion_grades Postgres table; the grading pass judges past suggestions at the grading_horizons_days (~1wk/1mo/1qtr); per-stock observations/seasonality live in stock_observations. GATED numeric auto-tuning (sizing/score weights) is allowed ONLY after auto_tune_after_graded_calls (50) graded calls in a bucket — until then judgment-only, documented not active. This is the honest version of 'learn from day 1 / get smarter over time' — memory + self-review over an LLM agent, NOT a trained price-prediction model (deliberately out of scope; such models overfit and mislead at this stage).

scoring._note: Transparent 0-100 stock quality/risk score, shown as a small tag (e.g. '76/100 low-med risk'). It is a SUMMARY of quality+risk, NOT a buy signal — the multi-role lens still makes the call. Valuation forgives a high P/E when growth is strong (PEG-style), like NVDA. Broad ETFs are diversified, so the score is skipped/noted rather than computed like a single stock. Qualitative risk flags (e.g. customer concentration) are best-effort from news; the score records which inputs it had and degrades gracefully when one is missing.

deep_dives._note: Inspired by the Anthropic-finance reel, built on FREE data ($0). Weekly catch-up = the first brief of each week (Monday) summarizes last week's news/ratings/filings + this week's earnings dates for watchlist + holdings. Two deeper checks are ON-DEMAND skills: 'equity-research' (plain-English research note re-checking a stock's thesis) and 'earnings-review' (earnings results digest; paste a transcript for a fuller call digest — full transcripts are paid, so the free version covers actual-vs-expected, guidance, and reaction). Most useful once you own stocks; usable now on watchlist names for learning.

guardrails.rule: Suggestion-only. The agent must never place, modify, or cancel a trade.
